name: Lakes Circuit Trail
name_original: Circuit des lacs — boucle — course à pied depuis Troncens
description: This moderate 5.7-mile (9.2 km) jogging loop from Troncens offers varied terrain and scenic views of Lac de Monpardiac and Cabournieu Reserv
region_intro: Jogging around Troncens offers a variety of routes through its gently rolling countryside. The region is characterized by open landscapes, rural paths, and occasional water features, providing a serene backdrop for running. Elevation gains are generally modest, making many routes accessible for different fitness levels. The area's trails connect several local villages and natural points of interest. Best jogging routes around Troncens The most popular jogging route is Lakes Circuit Trail, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of local water bodies. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Marciac, an easy 2.9 miles (4.7 km) path. This route provides a straightforward run through the local surroundings of Marciac. Local runners also love the Marciac – Marciac loop from Marciac, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 50 minutes. Jogging around Troncens is defined by open countryside, rural paths, and gentle hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging running experiences. The routes in Troncens are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 130 runners have used komoot to explore Troncens's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Troncens?
A: There are over 25 dedicated running routes around Troncens, offering a variety of options for different fitness levels. These include easy, moderate, and challenging trails.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or families in Troncens?
A: Yes, Troncens offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners or a family outing. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35666084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Villecomtal-sur-Arros&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 4.3 km long and features minimal elevation gain, providing a gentle introduction to the region's trails.

Q: What are some challenging long-distance running trails in the Troncens area?
A: For runners seeking a challenge, Troncens has longer and more demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26211977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Betplan&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 16.2 km trail with significant elevation changes, offering a robust workout through the rural landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Troncens?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Troncens are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197136833/lakes-circuit-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lakes Circuit Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route, approximately 9.2 km, offering scenic views of local water bodies.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails around Troncens?
A: The running trails around Troncens are characterized by gently rolling countryside, open landscapes, and rural paths. You'll often encounter serene water features and pass through charming local villages, providing a peaceful and picturesque backdrop for your run.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see along the running routes?
A: While running in the Troncens area, you might encounter several points of interest. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6364610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marciac&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight, known for its jazz festival. Other nearby attractions include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6047807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Our Lady of the Assumption of Marciac&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3464685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Sever-de-Rustan Abbey&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored as part of a longer outing.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Troncens?
A: The running trails in Troncens are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners frequently praise the region's varied terrain, from easy loops to more challenging distances, and the tranquil, open countryside that makes for an enjoyable experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Troncens?
A: Troncens offers pleasant running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, ideal for exploring the rural paths. Summer mornings and evenings are also great for a run, while winter can offer crisp, clear days, though some paths might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass through or near the town of Marciac?
A: Yes, Marciac is a central point for several running routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41585064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Marciac&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.7 km path directly from the town, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41586400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marciac – Marciac loop from Marciac&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 7.9 km run through the surrounding rural landscapes.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of running routes in Troncens?
A: The running routes in Troncens cater to all levels. You'll find a good mix, with 4 easy routes, 22 moderate routes, and 2 difficult routes. The terrain generally features modest elevation gains, making many trails accessible while still offering options for more experienced runners.

Q: Are the running trails in Troncens well-maintained?
A: The trails in Troncens are primarily rural paths and country roads. While generally well-maintained, conditions can vary with weather, especially after rain. Runners often find them to be clear and suitable for jogging, offering a natural and authentic experience of the countryside.
tips: Wonderful resevoir set in a large open space with picnic benches for taking a break.
This is a beautiful lake surrounded by hills which give it a dramatic air.

faqs.question: Where does the running loop start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Circuit des lacs loop conveniently starts behind the town hall in Troncens. Parking is generally available at this location.

faqs.question: Is this running loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While rated as 'moderate' for jogging, the trail has varied terrain with some inclines and declines, including a 'fairly significant elevation gain' in sections. Beginners should be prepared for a moderately undulating path rather than a flat one, combining paved and unpaved surfaces.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for running?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring sections of 'voie communale goudronnée' (tarmac communal road), 'chemin de terre' (dirt track), and paths through woods and along the lake. You can expect a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, with some hilly sections.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the running loop?
faqs.answer: The loop is approximately 9.2 km (5.7 miles) long. For jogging, it typically takes around 1 hour and 6 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on individual pace and stops.

faqs.question: What scenic views or points of interest can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You will experience 'superbes paysages vallonnés' (superb hilly landscapes) and serene natural environments. Key highlights include the tranquil Lac de Monpardiac and the Cabournieu Reservoir. The route also takes you through valleys and woods, offering beautiful views of the surrounding area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed in various seasons. Spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, so consider early morning or late afternoon runs. Winter running is possible, but check local conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Circuit des lacs — loop — running from Troncens dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near wildlife, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and run this loop trail.

faqs.question: Does this running route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the PR de Troncens, Chemin de Jacotte, Chemin du Lac, Chemin du Bois du Mède, and Chemin de Hustaraille.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like picnic areas or restrooms along the way?
faqs.answer: There are picnic tables available by the Lac de Monpardiac, providing a nice spot for a break. Restroom facilities are not explicitly mentioned along the trail itself, so it's advisable to plan accordingly.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or accommodation options near the starting point in Troncens?
faqs.answer: Troncens is a small commune, but you may find local cafes or accommodation options in the village or nearby towns. It's recommended to check local listings or inquire in advance for specific services.
